examples.title: Demonstrating Constructor/Destructor Order in Inheritance
examples.description: Shows construction from base to derived and destruction from derived to base
examples.explanation: Constructors execute in order: Base → Derived. Destructors execute in reverse: Derived → Base. This ensures base class portion is valid before derived part uses it, and derived resources are cleaned before base.

examples.title: Virtual Destructor Demonstration
examples.description: Shows why virtual destructor is essential for polymorphic deletion
examples.explanation: Without virtual ~Animal(), only Animal destructor would be called when delete ptr executes, causing Dog-specific resources to leak. Virtual ensures proper cleanup of derived class first.

examples.title: Copy Constructor Invocation Scenarios
examples.description: Demonstrates three common scenarios where copy constructor is called
examples.explanation: Three scenarios shown: (1) Passing object to function by value triggers copy constructor, (2) Returning object by value triggers copy constructor for return value, (3) Initializing new object with existing object triggers copy constructor. Destructors called in reverse order of construction.

examples.title: Global vs Static vs Local Object Lifecycle
examples.description: Demonstrates timing differences between different storage class objects
examples.explanation: Shows that: (1) Global object constructed before main() and destroyed after main() ends, (2) Static local constructed on first function call only, (3) Local constructed and destroyed each time function is called. Static local is never constructed again on subsequent calls.

examples.title: Member Object Construction Order
examples.description: Demonstrates that member objects are constructed in declaration order, not initializer list order
examples.explanation: Members are constructed in declaration order (a then b), NOT initializer list order. Destructors run in reverse: Container→B→A. Even if you try to initialize b before a in initializer list, a is still constructed first.
